ddls:
CREATE TABLE "event_logs" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event log entry */,
  "computer_name" TEXT /* Name of the computer generating the log */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of the log entry (e.g., windows:evtx:record) */,
  "event_datetime" TEXT /* Date and time when the event occurred */,
  "display_name" TEXT /* Display name of the log source */,
  "event_identifier" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the specific event */,
  "event_level" INTEGER /* Level of the event (e.g., informational, warning, error) */,
  "label" TEXT /* Label for categorizing the event */,
  "message_identifier" REAL /* Message identifier associated with the event */,
  "offset" INTEGER /* Offset value for the log entry */,
  "parser" TEXT /* Parser used to interpret the log entry */,
  "record_number" INTEGER /* Record number of the log entry */,
  "recovered" INTEGER /* Indicates if the entry was recovered */,
  "sha256_hash" TEXT /* SHA-256 hash of the log entry for integrity checks */,
  "source_long" TEXT /* Long source name of the log */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the source generating the log */,
  "source_short" TEXT /* Short source name or abbreviation */,
  "strings" TEXT /* Strings associated with the log entry */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Primary timestamp for the event */,
  "timestamp_desc" TEXT /* Description of the timestamp */,
  "user_sid" TEXT /* User SID associated with the event */,
  "log_source_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the log source */,
  "event_category_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the event category */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Specific type of the event (e.g., security, application) */,
  "event_keywords" TEXT /* Keywords associated with the event for better search and filtering */,
  "event_data" TEXT /* JSON field to store additional event data in a structured format */,
  "is_archived" INTEGER /* Indicates if the log has been archived */,
  "retention_policy_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the data retention policy */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_logs_event_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_category_id") REFERENCES event_categories ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_logs_event_level FOREIGN KEY ("event_level") REFERENCES event_levels ("level_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_logs_retention_policy_id FOREIGN KEY ("retention_policy_id") REFERENCES retention_policies ("policy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_logs_log_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("log_source_id") REFERENCES log_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_logs_parser FOREIGN KEY ("parser") REFERENCES parsers ("parser_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_logs_computer_name FOREIGN KEY ("computer_name") REFERENCES computers ("computer_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "computers" (
  "computer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each computer */,
  "computer_name" TEXT /* Name of the computer */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the computer */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical or logical location of the computer */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the computer */,
  "os_version" TEXT /* Operating system version of the computer */,
  "last_update" TEXT /* Last time the computer was updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the computer (e.g., active, decommissioned) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or team the computer belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("computer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the event category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "severity" INTEGER /* Severity level of the category (e.g., 1 for low, 5 for high) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_levels" (
  "level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event level */,
  "level_name" TEXT /* Name of the event level (e.g., informational, error) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event level */,
  "color_code" TEXT /* Color code for visual representation of the event level */,
  "icon" TEXT /* Icon or symbol for visual representation of the event level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "parsers" (
  "parser_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parser */,
  "parser_name" TEXT /* Name of the parser */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the parsing method */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the parser */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last time the parser was updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parser_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst) */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last time the user logged in */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the event log being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the event log */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the access event */,
  "action_type" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the access request */,
  "is_successful" INTEGER /* Indicates if the access was successful */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES event_logs ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "log_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the log source */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of the source (e.g., system, application) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the log source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "retention_policies" (
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each retention policy */,
  "policy_name" TEXT /* Name of the retention policy */,
  "retention_period" INTEGER /* Number of days the logs should be retained */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the retention policy */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("policy_id")
)
